title: Guidonia tomentosa
taxonomy: Guidonia tomentosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Brazil and is a part of the genus Guidonia. The classification of Guidonia tomentosa places it within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the order Gentianales. This classification further breaks down into the family Rubiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants known for their diverse range of species. Guidonia tomentosa is closely related to other members of the Rubiaceae family, which includes plants like coffee and gardenias.

Its bark and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds.
